title: campyloneurum serpentinum
intro: Campyloneurum serpentinum, also known as serpent's tongue, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The leaves of the serpent's tongue fern contain flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. Campyloneurum serpentinum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
